note: Provisional FT-001 through FT-010 assigned for Figma plugin migration. IDs confirmed by FLOW-36 Phase A. Upgraded to schema v2.0 (productScope discriminator added).

features.name: DesignToCodeExtraction
features.description: Converts selected Figma frames into clean HTML and CSS code via an 18-step CSS extraction pipeline with semantic element classification.
portingConstraints: Figma node type system (FRAME, TEXT, RECTANGLE, ELLIPSE, etc.) must be mapped to target platform's node model
fills, strokes, effects, layoutMode, children property access requires platform-specific adapter
Image extraction via exportAsync() needs equivalent bitmap export API on target platform
Color space: Figma uses 0-1 RGB floats; must convert to CSS hex/rgba in extraction layer
cornerSmoothing and strokeMiterLimit have no 1:1 CSS equivalents — approximations needed

features.name: InteractiveElementPreview
features.description: Live clickable HTML preview inside the plugin UI showing generated code with element selection, hover states, and pixel-perfect rendering.
portingConstraints: Requires an iframe or browser DOM context for React rendering
CSS class names use Figma node IDs — must be re-keyed to target platform's node identifiers
dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage requires sanitization review per platform security policy

features.name: LiveStyleEditor
features.description: Click any element in the preview to inspect and live-edit CSS properties, with automatic re-rendering and code regeneration.
portingConstraints: Operates on extracted CSS settings (name/value pairs) — platform-agnostic once extraction layer is ported
Element selection state managed in React component tree; must integrate with target platform's UI state model

features.name: AICodeEnhancement
features.description: Uses Claude/Anthropic API to take generated HTML/CSS and produce full-stack code (React, Express, FastAPI, Vue, etc.) with 7 pre-built stack templates.
portingConstraints: API key stored via figma.clientStorage — must use platform equivalent (localStorage, secure storage, etc.)
Frame image export for vision template requires bitmap export capability on target platform
Prompt templates (7 stacks) are portable as-is; stack selection UI needs platform-specific wrapper
CORS and CSP constraints differ per platform; API proxy may be required

features.name: DesignFidelityCorrection
features.description: Vision-based template that compares original design image against generated code and uses Claude to fix discrepancies in colors, spacing, typography, and layouts.
portingConstraints: Requires frame export to JPEG via exportAsync() at 0.5x scale — equivalent bitmap export needed on target
Canvas API used for image pre-compression before API submission
Requires vision-capable AI API (Claude 3.5+); feature degrades gracefully without image

features.name: DesignGenerationFromPrompt
features.description: AI-driven iterative canvas generation: user describes a design in natural language; DALL-E 3 generates a reference image; Claude applies delta operations to build the design directly in the canvas.
portingConstraints: Delta operation executor (op-executor.ts) uses Figma-specific node creation API (createFrame, createText, createRectangle, appendChild) — full rewrite required per platform
Node resolution via figma.getNodeByIdAsync() and figma.loadAllPagesAsync() — platform-specific
Frame export for visual feedback loop requires exportAsync() equivalent
Requires two AI APIs simultaneously: OpenAI DALL-E 3 (image gen) + Claude (vision comparison + delta generation)
Session state machine (idle → generating-image → comparing → executing → loop) is platform-agnostic
platformIncompatibilities: miro: Canvas creation API differs substantially; delta op model needs full reimplementation
webflow: DOM-based editor; node creation approach is HTML/CSS not graphic primitives

features.name: LayoutOptimizer
features.description: Converts absolute-positioned layouts to flex/grid layouts with semantic gap values by analyzing child spacing patterns in the extracted element tree.
portingConstraints: Operates on extracted CSS property data only — no Figma API calls; fully portable
ImproveAbsoluteToGap algorithm is pure TypeScript; can be extracted as shared library

features.name: GenerationSessionMemory
features.description: Persists up to 10 generation sessions per scene with feedback scoring, user ratings, and metadata for iterative refinement of AI-generated designs.
portingConstraints: Storage layer uses figma.clientStorage (async key-value, persistent per user per file) — must swap to platform equivalent (localStorage, indexedDB, platform storage API)
IStorage interface already abstracts the storage layer — only the concrete adapter needs porting
SessionEntry schema (html, css, score, feedback, source, timestamp) is platform-agnostic

features.name: ElementTreeInspector
features.description: Hierarchical view of the extracted element structure with breadcrumb navigation, showing parent/child relationships and source element types.
portingConstraints: Displays Figma node type names (FRAME, TEXT, etc.) — labels must be mapped to target platform terminology
Tree rendering is pure React; portable to any browser-based plugin context

features.name: ScenePackageExport
features.description: Exports the selected scene as a multi-file bundle including standalone HTML, CSS, debug history JSON, and element tree JSON for version control and testing.
portingConstraints: Uses browser File API (Blob, URL.createObjectURL, <a download>) — available in most browser-hosted plugin contexts
CSP restrictions in Figma iframe may block downloads; fallback to clipboard copy implemented
Figma-specific frame screenshot export optional; bundle degrades gracefully without it
platformIncompatibilities: vscode: Extension context has no browser download API; must use workspace file write instead
